meta.resumePath: LinkedIn primary (https://www.linkedin.com/in/max-seto-pmp-75ab992b/); ./assets/docs/ checked — no separate resume file
meta.linkedInNote: Profile fetched via automated fetch; facts aligned to public LinkedIn content.

overallMatch.summary: Strong Greater China presales leadership and enterprise SaaS adoption credibility (Workday Head of Presales Greater China; Oracle) align with stakeholder engagement and digital platform change. The role is anchored in life insurance agency distribution, agent analytics, and regulated insurer operations—transferable leadership with a moderate industry/channel gap to close in interviews.

granularScores.rationale: Strong enterprise SaaS, solution design, and analytics-adjacent experience; insurance agency systems and IA regulatory depth not explicit on profile.
21+ years in software presales/consulting vs. field agency distribution and insurer-owned digital products—strong leadership, different domain.
PMP® and delivery discipline; formal degree not detailed on LinkedIn; insurance qualifications (e.g., IIQE) not listed.
Hong Kong SAR matches HK–AIA Hong Kong–Macau posting and regional collaboration.
Vendor and regional pivots with C-level engagement support a cross-industry move with a learning curve on agency.
Principal role can lead to insurance digital leadership if domain gaps close; depends on outcomes and mobility.
Senior vendor comp may differ from insurer bands; validate Principal (Senior Manager) expectations in process.

jobSpecificScores.rationale: Less direct agency sales-force tenure vs. district visits and agent productivity levers in the posting.
Enterprise SaaS adoption and journey framing; map explicitly to AIA+ and agency digital stack.
Strong business analysis; must translate KPIs to agent behavior, not only enterprise HR metrics.
AI-in-work and GTM innovation exposure; ideation workshops fit presales leadership pattern.
Regulated financial services depth to verify; prepare HK IA and distribution conduct basics.
Discovery, PoCs, and C-level engagement map to internal partners and facilitation.
Driving platform adoption and roadmaps matches both presales and agency digital mandates.

jobRequirements.evidence: Led Greater China presales; enterprise-scale adoption and discovery programs.

jobRequirements.evidence: Workday ecosystem exposure to AI-in-work narratives; not AIA-specific.

jobRequirements.evidence: Broad SaaS/ERP/HCM platform fluency; agency portal specifics not on profile.

jobRequirements.requirement: Partner with business & IT on requirements and delivery
jobRequirements.evidence: Cross-functional presales, PoCs, and integration-heavy projects.

jobRequirements.requirement: Field perspective (e.g., district visits) informed by data
jobRequirements.evidence: Regional field engagement in vendor context; insurance districts not explicit.
Analytics-adjacent solution work; agent-behavior metrics to be demonstrated.

jobRequirements.requirement: Innovation workshops & open innovation with agency
jobRequirements.evidence: GTM and workshop-style customer engagement; innovation vocabulary.
Enterprise delivery discipline; HK insurance-specific IA depth not listed.

strengths: Head of Presales Greater China — C-level and enterprise discovery, demos, PoCs.
PMP® and structured delivery — fits programmatic digital rollouts.
Hong Kong SAR base — matches posting location.
Digital transformation fluency — SaaS, HCM/ERP, integration, adoption.
Go-to-market and innovation facilitation — maps to ideation and cross-functional partnering.

gaps.gap: Direct life insurance agency distribution experience
gaps.recommendation: Prepare AIA Premier Agency, MDRT, and agent funnel metrics; map 2–3 enterprise adoption wins to field agents.
Skim IA themes on digital distribution; use safe examples of compliant rollouts from enterprise SaaS.

gaps.gap: AIA-specific digital stack (e.g., AIA+, agency portals)
gaps.recommendation: Build a concise one-page homework view on public channels and agent digital touchpoints.

interviewPrepFocus: Why AIA — purpose tied to agency enablement, not vendor pitch.
Agent adoption story — distributed users, metrics, change management.
Responsible AI in regulated insurance — coaching, next-best-action, governance.
Compliance — privacy/security baked into enterprise rollouts.
Metrics — MAU, activation, retention, productivity applied to agency.
actionableRecommendations: Reframe LinkedIn headline/summary toward adoption, digital programs, and distributed user success (agency-adjacent).
Mirror posting vocabulary in STAR stories: digital agent engagement, analytics foundation, district insights, innovation workshops.
Prepare one AIA-specific talking point: AIA+, agency digital priorities, competitor comparison.
Confirm IIQE / licensing expectations with recruiter for Principal scope.
Network thoughtfully with AIA digital/agency/data contacts for context.

highlights.executiveSummary:
## Executive Summary

**AIA Group Limited** is the **largest independent publicly listed pan-Asian life insurance group**, with presence in **18 markets** including **Hong Kong SAR** and **Macau SAR**. The Group traces its origins to **1919** in Shanghai, leads **Asia (ex-Japan)** life premiums, and reported **total assets of US$328 billion** (Group disclosure, mid-2025). AIA serves **tens of millions** of customers through **agents, partners, and digital channels** with life, accident & health, savings, and employee benefits.

The **Principal — Agency Digital & Innovation** role sits in **AIA Hong Kong & Macau’s** **agency distribution** engine—driving **digital agent engagement**, **analytics**, **platform evolution**, and **innovation** (including **AI**) within **Insurance Authority**–regulated distribution. Public messaging highlights **No. 1 in new business policies** (multi-year), **Premier Agency** and **MDRT** leadership, and **AIA+** as a **digital engagement** pillar.

### Leadership & platform (snapshot)

- **CEO (AIA Hong Kong & Macau):** Public materials reference **Alger Fung** — verify current title at interview.
- **Platform:** **AIA+** and **agency digital** capabilities as levers for **agent productivity** and **customer retention**.

### Hong Kong / Macau

- **Market:** Mature, competitive life market; **digital conduct** and **customer protection** matter for distribution.
- **Culture:** **Agency**-centric heritage; **customer centricity** and **brand trust** recur in press releases. The **Role** in **Location** rewards leaders who combine **digital** execution with **regulatory** awareness.
highlights.leadership: Public CEO statements emphasize customer centricity and sustained market leadership. Agency production (MDRT/COT/TOT) is a visible pillar of HK competitiveness.
highlights.products: Life, health (VHIS-related propositions), savings/investment-linked, group insurance, MPF/ORSO/Macau pension adjacency; AIA+ mobile app for service and engagement.
highlights.culture: Premier Agency and high-performance sales culture; regulated insurer norms (compliance, conduct, risk) alongside digital innovation narratives.
highlights.recommendation: Candidates should speak agency outcomes (productivity, retention, adoption) and regulated delivery—not only enterprise SaaS feature lists.
